analysis_description: Round-3 tapes days 0–2: Black–Scholes implied vol vs m_t=log(K/S)/sqrt(T) with T from round3description (CSV day 0,1,2 → 8,7,6 d at open) and intraday DTE wind-down (timestamps 0..999900 step 100 → ~1 day per session) per round3work/plotting/original_method/combined_analysis/plot_iv_smile_round3.py. Fitted quadratic IV(m_t) per subsample; mean quadratic coefficient a>0 (upward curvature); mean wing IV − core IV > 0; bid-ask width on VEV_5200 and extract mids from CSV.
used_to_decide: Positive mean IV quadratic term and wing−core IV premium support fading rich wings vs smooth core; spread filters sized vs ~3-tick VEV and ~5-tick extract widths. Chose v0 thresholds then refined cheap-wing filter in v2 after v1 sweep hurt PnL.

analysis_description: Implicit grid over thresholds / hedge / m_t filter (trader_v1 vs v0 backtests); confirmed full hedge with tight |m_t| gate reduced wing shorting too much and worsened aggregate PnL versus v0.
used_to_decide: Steered v2 back toward v0 structure with IV mispricing guard (time-value ratio) instead of m_t-only gate.

analysis_description: Compared v2 vs v0 PnL decomposition in backtest logs: large losses on VEV_4500 longs in v0; v2’s time-value filter reduced extract hedge bleed while keeping wing shorts.
used_to_decide: Kept v2 as preferred active curvature implementation for next tuning.

analysis_description: v3 sell-only sweep: smile residuals rarely exceeded lowered RICH threshold with spread filter; activity collapsed to incidental position on VEV_6000.
used_to_decide: Sell-only at this threshold is too passive; future iterations should blend sell-rich with selective inversion or lower RICH_THR with care for fill quality.

analysis_description: Per-strike IV/Greek tradeability study on ROUND_3 tapes (subsample every 100 ticks): BS implied vol from mids (r=0), per-timestamp quadratic IV(m_t) residuals, plus delta/vega summaries by strike. DTE/TTE mapping from round3description interpreted as historical CSV day 0/1/2 -> 8/7/6 at open, with intraday wind-down ~1 day over the session.
used_to_decide: Found VEV_4000/4500 have near-zero time-value fraction and noisier residuals, while 5400/5500 retain meaningful vega and tradable residual structure; informed focused-wing variants v4/v5 and lower-wing long suppression in v6.

analysis_description: Variant comparison on same curvature thesis: v4 (focused 5400/5500 with short-cover) was too lossy due to hedge bleed; v5 (stricter + no cheap-wing longs) turned fully passive; v6 (active v2-parent with lower-wing long suppression) remained active but underperformed.
used_to_decide: Best realized result remains conservative/passy v5 at total 0, but that confirms current residual triggers are too weak under worse fills; next work should re-center residual estimation/entry timing while keeping tradeability filters.

analysis_description: v6 vs v2: blocking 4000/4500 cheap longs reduced some loss tail but total PnL worsened vs v2; still valid as a parent for strike-scoped experiments.
used_to_decide: Preferred parent for next grid remained v2 (−751) over v6 (−2326).

analysis_description: Signed residual check on tapes (50-tick step, time-value filter): IV−quadratic(m_t) at 5400/5500 is almost always negative (short side of parabola); 6000 often positive. Same TTE mapping as round3description + intraday wind-down. Correlation artifact: analyze_wing_resid_vs_extract_v7.py → wing_resid_vs_extract_v7.json (Δlog S vs Δresid, weak pooled r ~0.05–0.09 for 5400).
used_to_decide: Explains v7–v10 zero activity when entry required resid>RICH on 5400/5500 only; led to v11 signed-strike split (short 6000 rich, long 5400/5500 cheap).

analysis_description: n/a — v8 grid vs v7 (same structural issue).
used_to_decide: No change to signed-residual conclusion.

analysis_description: n/a — v9 lower RICH/EXIT on 5400/5500-only short rule.
used_to_decide: Still no positive RICH events at 5400/5500 at scale under quadratic fit.

analysis_description: n/a — v10 lower-threshold grid vs v8.
used_to_decide: Same as iteration 9.

analysis_description: v11 backtest: signed-strike book triggered but total −3357 (extract hedge drag). v2 still best in-folder; pivot documented without abandoning smile-residual concept.
used_to_decide: Next iterations should branch from v2: strike-specific or cubic smile, or smaller option clips / lower HEDGE_FRAC to reduce extract churn.

analysis_description: analyze_quad_vs_cubic_v12.py → quad_vs_cubic_smile_v12.json: cubic IV(m_t) lowers in-sample RMSE vs quadratic but mean |resid| on wing strikes is larger; with v2’s fixed RICH/CHEAP, v12 never entered (0 PnL).
used_to_decide: Kept quadratic for v13–v15 parameter sweeps; cubic would need threshold recalibration.

analysis_description: v13 vs v2: lower HEDGE_FRAC (0.62) and MAX_LEG 10 cut extract bleed; total +759 vs v2 −751 on days 0–2 (worse-match backtest).
used_to_decide: Confirmed hedge as main loss channel; extended to v15.

analysis_description: v14 HEDGE 0.50 vs v13 0.62: int-rounded extract hedge matched v13 path in this run (same total 759).
used_to_decide: Use hedge fractions that change int(round(hedge*d_net)) when grid-searching.

analysis_description: v15 HEDGE 0.28: option legs match v13 pattern but extract PnL mostly near zero (small d_net rounds to 0 hedge), total 3010 on days 0–2 — new best in this strategy folder.
used_to_decide: Prefer v15 line for next micro-sweeps (RICH/CHEAP, optional cap on extract hedge when |d_net| large).

analysis_description: analyze_wing_resid_thresholds_v16.py → wing_resid_thresholds_v16.json: on subsample (step 50), max |quadratic IV resid| over wing strikes is ≥0.012 at ~80.7% of timestamps, ≥0.014 at ~68% — guides RICH sweep (higher RICH cuts activity).
used_to_decide: v16 RICH=0.014 was too tight (total 1565 vs v15); v17 RICH=0.010 + CHEAP=-0.022 beat v15 (3659).

analysis_description: v17 backtest: looser RICH and stricter CHEAP than v15; option-centric PnL with light extract (like v15); new best total 3659 on days 0–2.
used_to_decide: Next parent: trader_v17.py; micro-sweep CHEAP in [-0.020,-0.024] or MAX_LEG 8–12.

analysis_description: vega_weighted_resid_v18.json: vega-weighted wing residual mean is negative (~-0.0105), indicating aggregate cheap-wing bias versus quadratic core; tested stricter cheap threshold and slightly tighter rich threshold.
used_to_decide: v18 improved over v15 but under v17; stricter entries reduced day1 participation too much.

analysis_description: Risk sizing sweep from best parent v17: reduce MAX_LEG 10->8 while keeping thresholds fixed.
used_to_decide: v19 (3547) remained close but below v17 (3659), suggesting clip reduction under-uses profitable day2 signals.

analysis_description: Mixed-parent test (older v15 mechanics + v17 thresholds) with MAX_LEG 12; keeps low hedge and larger clip to exploit day2 edge while still curbing extract drag.
used_to_decide: v20 is new best (3750), so next iterations should parent from v20 and tune MAX_LEG/thresholds around this regime.

analysis_description: analyze_spread_on_smile_triggers_v21.py → spread_on_smile_triggers_v21.json: when v20-style RICH/CHEAP would fire on wing strikes, median bid-ask width is about 1 tick (rich and cheap subsamples); informs spread filter tuning.
used_to_decide: Loosening MIN_SPREAD_SKIP 6→4 or tightening 6→8 left PnL unchanged (3750): active path still passes at width ≤6; v22 larger MAX_LEG hurt (2984) via larger d_net and extract hedge.

analysis_description: v22: MAX_LEG 14 vs v20 12 increases option clip and thus |d_net|, so int-rounded extract hedge engages more (day1/2 extract negative), lowering total.
used_to_decide: Prefer MAX_LEG 12 or lower with HEDGE 0.28; avoid upsizing unless hedge is reduced further.

analysis_description: v23 stricter spread cap 8: no PnL change vs v20 because executed paths on this tape already had spread ≤6 at entry.
used_to_decide: Spread filter is not the binding constraint here; keep simpler v20/v21/v23 tie at 3750.

analysis_description: v24 RICH 0.009: no path change vs v20 (same 3750); dominant PnL still from VEV_5400 sequence where residual dynamics already tripped under 0.010.
used_to_decide: Next sweep: CHEAP grid or hydrogel tilt only if extract hedge re-engages; else stay at v20-class params.

analysis_description: Requested family10 regime analysis: stressed if extract spread>=6 OR extract depth<=110; normal otherwise. Frequency normal~58.7%, stressed~41.3%. Fit quality differs sharply: normal mean RMSE~0.0116 and wing |resid|~0.0077 vs stressed RMSE~0.169 and wing |resid|~0.153. Implemented regime-dependent smile_quadratic_logm_wls calibration set (normal all strikes, stressed core-only) and ran worse-fill backtest.
used_to_decide: Regime split is real, but v25 produced heavy fills in VEV_6000/6500 and large losses (-29.6k), far below both local best (3.75k) and the ~800k bar.

analysis_description: Stricter stress trigger (v26) increased stressed/core-only calibration usage and further concentrated fills in far wings (fill summary: larger VEV_6000/6500 quantity than v25), worsening losses to -53.9k.
used_to_decide: This family10 regime-dependent core-only calibration appears over-aggressive for current signal thresholds in this branch; keep documented pivot result and revert to prior best branch (v20/v21/v23/v24 class) unless re-parameterizing entry logic substantially.

analysis_description: Duplicate-check-driven pivot within family10: moved stressed calibration from core-only to core+near-wings and capped WLS weights to avoid far-wing domination seen in v25/v26 fill summaries. Performance improved from v25/v26 but still negative (-1500).
used_to_decide: Core+near-wing stressed set materially reduced far-wing fill concentration vs v25/v26, but still below prior non-family10 branch best.

analysis_description: Conservative family10 variant (stricter thresholds + lower WLS cap) lifted total to +1723 with lower fill counts than v27, but still below v20-class 3750 and far below ~800k bar.
used_to_decide: Family10 regime-WLS variations under current assumptions remain inferior to existing best branch settings; retain as documented non-duplicate exploration.

analysis_description: underlying_response_spread_iv_v29.json: voucher response beta to extract drops strongly by strike (e.g., high near ITM, weak far OTM); lag/lead betas near zero -> mostly contemporaneous response. Shock spreads widen little for dominant wing leg. Used this to define beta-bucket clip scaling in v29.
used_to_decide: Response-aware clip scaling did not change realized path vs v20 on this tape (same 3750), indicating executed path dominated by one strike already at effective cap.

analysis_description: Same response analysis suggested asymmetry mostly in shock timing, so v30 tightened cheap-long entry after large extract moves. This slightly improved total to 3754 with similar fill footprint.
used_to_decide: v30 is marginally best in branch; still far below ~800k bar, but this is a meaningful directed tweak rather than random nudging.

analysis_description: v31: Greek leg trim; submission-only stat parse (fill_summary) matches v30: 118 submission trades, VEV_6000 900 qty, total 3754.5. Regenerated via summarize_backtest_log.py (submission rows = non-empty buyer/seller in tradeHistory).
used_to_decide: v31 is not a PnL duplicate of v20 but matches v30 path on this tape; keep v30 as reference for shock experiments.

analysis_description: v32: far-OTM cheap block on |dS| shock; fill_summary identical to v30/v31 (118 submission rows, 3754.5) — one-tick shock gate does not alter which orders fill.
used_to_decide: Need persistent or threshold-level shock state (v33) or other lever; simple shock bit ineffective.

analysis_description: v33: shock_left persistence (3 ticks) for far-OTM cheap block. Parsed log: same 118 submission trades and 3754.5 as v30; shock persistence also does not change filled submission path on this run.
used_to_decide: If far-OTM filter never binds at submission, pivot to per-strike CHEAP/clip (v34–v36) or accept VEV_6000 PnL comes from a different trade category.

analysis_description: v34: per-strike CHEAP -0.026 on 6000/6500 only. fill_summary: unchanged vs v30 (3754.5, VEV_6000 -450, 900 submission qty). Tighter cheap threshold in band still does not change which submissions cross.
used_to_decide: Grid step documented; try clip-only (v35) and hard no long (v36).

analysis_description: v35: MAX_LEG 8 for cheap long on 6000/6500 only. fill_summary again identical: 900 VEV_6000 submission qty, -450 on VEV_6000, total 3754.5 — implied orders were already at or below 8 or clip was not the binding constraint in the worst-fill path we measure.
used_to_decide: v36: disable far-OTM long branch entirely to test hypothesis that PnL bucket is not cheap-long.

analysis_description: v36: NO_CHEAP_LONG_KS = {6000,6500}. fill_summary still 3754.5, VEV_6000 -450, 75 trades / 900 qty on VEV_6000 in submission parse — identical to v30. EXACT_DUPLICATE_CHECK: code differs (cheap branch skipped for 6000/6500) but 118-submission path and PnL match v30‑class; not byte-duplicate, functionally same outcome, so the ~450 loss on VEV_6000 is not removed by turning off that branch (likely not driven by our cheap-long intent or non-submission path dominates tape PnL at end state).
used_to_decide: Next iteration must disambiguate: split submission vs full tape PnL for VEV_6000, or test rich-short-only / different wing set, rather than more cheap-far-OTM gates that do not change submissions.

analysis_description: Parsed backtest_v30 tradeHistory: all 75 submission-tagged VEV_6000 rows have quantity>0 and seller='SUBMISSION' (buy flow); VEV_5400 rows have buyer='SUBMISSION'. So v34–v36 conditions on the cheap-long elif branch never applied to the fills we counted. v37 removes strikes 6000/6500 from WING_KS only (smile still uses all vouchers with valid IV). fill_summary_v37: total 4204.5 (+450 vs v30), VEV_6000/6500 PnL 0, n_submission_trade_rows 43 (was 118), no VEV_6000 in submission_qty.
used_to_decide: New branch best is v37; far-OTM was execution-only drag under worse fills, not fixable by CHEAP gating. Next: optional re-add 6500 only if tape shows edge, or micro-tune RICH/CHEAP around v37's path.
